extraction.one_line_summary: PULLES Wout won the 18.4km ITT to Santa Tème, taking 16 seconds from Felipe and climbing into GC contention before mountain stages.
tactical_patterns: Sky deployed multiple ITT pacesetters (van Baarle) early to establish time benchmarks, classic depth tactic for GC defense.
Quickstep's Felipe took maximum risks in wet conditions but lost 16 seconds, revealing tactical gamble failed in changing weather.
Sanchez preserved GC lead by only 5 seconds over Yates, indicating Astana lacked ITT depth to defend yellow properly.

rider_signals.PULLES Wout: positive: first 2018 win suggests peak timing plus commentary notes climbing skills make him genuine GC outsider, not just ITT specialist
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: finished just behind GC contenders in ITT despite being pure climber, gained 10 seconds on Sanchez pre-mountains
rider_signals.SANCHEZ Luis Leon: negative: ranked only 7th in ITT as defending GC leader, retained yellow by minimal 5-second margin showing weakness
rider_signals.FELIPE Julien: negative: excessive risk-taking in wet ITT lost him 16 seconds to Pulles, tactical error before crucial mountain stages
